title: ENGINE MAINTENANCE — Positive Crankcase Ventilation (P.C.V.) Valve (Cont'd) / Changing P.C.V. Filter / Checking Vacuum Hoses and Connections / Checking Vapor Lines

figures_detected.description: Photograph showing ventilation hoses in the engine bay
Photograph showing P.C.V. filter location inside air cleaner housing
Photograph showing purge line and vapor line routing in engine bay

steps.text: Check hoses and hose connections for leaks.
Disconnect all hoses and clean with compressed air. If any hose cannot be freed of obstructions, replace.

content_blocks.text: Remove air cleaner cover and air cleaner filter, and replace P.C.V. filter.
Check vacuum hoses for improper attachment and for leaks, cracks, damage, loose connections, chafing and deterioration.
Refer to Vacuum Hose Drawing in ENGINE AND EMISSION CONTROL OVERALL SYSTEM in EF & EC section.

steps.text: Visually inspect vapor lines for improper attachment and for cracks, damage, loose connections, chafing and deterioration.
Inspect vacuum relief valve of fuel tank filler cap for clogging, sticking, etc.

content_blocks.text: Refer to EVAPORATIVE EMISSION CONTROL SYSTEM INSPECTION in EF & EC section.

cross_references: EF & EC — ENGINE AND EMISSION CONTROL OVERALL SYSTEM (Vacuum Hose Drawing)
EF & EC — EVAPORATIVE EMISSION CONTROL SYSTEM INSPECTION
